channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Superstition and mob violence in Tanzania: A growing concern
item.description: A recent wave of panic in Tanzania has revealed how deeply superstition can drive violence and fear in communities. In Songwe Region, rumours about ‘genital theft’ linked to witchcraft led to widespre...

item.title: Rajya Sabha Chairman Dismisses Opposition Motion to Remove Chief Election Commissioner
item.description: The Rajya Sabha chairman, CP Radhakrishnan, has rejected a notice submitted by the opposition seeking a motion to remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. In his ruling, Radhakrishnan clarifi...

item.title: Tanzania Authorities Target Illegal Online Cosmetic and Medicine Sales
item.description: Tanzania's regulatory bodies are intensifying efforts to combat the illegal sale of unregistered cosmetics and medicines through online platforms. This crackdown aims to protect public health and ensu...

item.title: The artistic and practical reasons behind masked performances in metal music
item.description: Many contemporary metal bands, from Sleep Token to Ghost and Slaughter to Prevail, choose to wear masks during performances, a practice that is growing in popularity. While masks create practical diff...

item.title: Perth parents granted bail after appealing convictions over daughter's malnutrition
item.description: Parents in Perth who were previously sentenced to jail for allegedly starving their young daughter, a ballet dancer, have been granted bail as they await a retrial. The case attracted significant atte...

item.title: Researchers Identify Astrocyte Pathway That Regulates Appetite and Fullness
item.description: A recent study from the University of Maryland and the University of Concepción reveals that the brain relies on more than traditional neurons to decide when you feel full. The research, published in ...

item.title: Iceland offers job to former Waitrose worker dismissed for intervening in shoplifting incident
item.description: Walker Smith, a 17-year employee at Waitrose, was dismissed after he tried to prevent a shoplifter from stealing Easter eggs at a Waitrose store in Clapham, London. Despite a brief struggle with the t...

item.title: Former Governor Sonko Condemns Death of Egg Vendor Brian Muendo in Nairobi
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko has expressed deep concern over the tragic death of 25-year-old egg vendor Brian Muendo, who was allegedly killed outside Soma Club in the Central Business District....

item.title: Iraq poised to rapidly resume oil exports if Strait of Hormuz reopens
item.description: Iraq’s oil industry has been severely disrupted due to the ongoing conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which has blocked significant oil and gas shipments. According to Bassem Abdul Kari...

item.title: Pakistan Expresses Support for Saudi Arabia Following Recent Attacks
item.description: Pakistan has once again showed full support for Saudi Arabia after recent attacks on energy facilities in the kingdom. The Foreign Office (FO) strongly condemned the attacks, labeling them as a seriou...

item.title: PMDC schedules NEB exam on 16 May for returning medical students
item.description: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) has announced that it will hold the National Equivalence Board (NEB) examination on 16 May 2026. This test is meant for Pakistani medical and dental stud...

item.title: Tanzania Introduces Stricter Energy Efficiency Standards for Electrical Appliances
item.description: Tanzania is entering a critical phase in its energy sector, introducing new regulations aimed at improving energy efficiency and reducing electricity consumption. Starting in the 2026/27 fiscal year, ...

item.title: Cameron Munster assesses Melbourne Storm’s early-season struggles after Panthers loss
item.description: Following a disappointing performance against the Penrith Panthers, Melbourne Storm star Cameron Munster has openly reflected on the club’s sluggish start to the 2026 NRL season. Speaking to reporters...

item.title: Kenyan Energy Officials Released on Bail Amid Fuel Import Investigation
item.description: Senior officials in Kenya’s energy sector are back on the streets after being released on Sh100,000 police bail pending further investigations related to alleged irregularities in fuel procurement. Th...

item.title: Global energy crisis looms as Strait of Hormuz remains closed amid Iran conflict
item.description: The ongoing U.S. and Israeli conflict with Iran, now in its sixth week, has triggered a severe energy supply disruption that threatens the global economy. Despite hopes for a quick resolution, the eff...

item.title: Eterna Plc Reports Significant Profit Growth in 2025
item.description: Na so Eterna Plc don announce their financial results for 2025, and dem don show serious profit jump! Dem make ₦7.27 billion profit before tax (PBT), which na 52.9% more than last year. Back in 2024, ...

item.title: Assam Assembly Elections 2026 Conclude Campaigning; Over 700 Candidates in the Fray
item.description: The state of Assam is all set for its Assembly elections on April 9, 2026, with the three-week long campaigning officially concluding on Tuesday. A total of 722 candidates are contesting for 126 assem...

item.title: Trump Confirms Iran Sanctions and Military Deadline Will Proceed as Planned
item.description: In an interview with Fox News, former President Donald Trump confirmed that the 'Iran deadline' he previously announced remains in effect, signaling that the United States will continue its firm stanc...

item.title: Clavano warns against premature release of ICI findings amid ongoing Ombudsman flood control probe
item.description: Assistant Ombudsman Mico Clavano expressed concerns that the premature release of findings from the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) could compromise the Office of the Ombudsman's inves...

item.title: Matt Baugh Takes Over as British High Commissioner to Kenya After Neil Wigan’s Tenure
item.description: The United Kingdom has appointed Matt Baugh as the new British High Commissioner to Kenya, taking over from Neil Wigan who left the Nairobi post in August 2025 to become Director General for Strategy ...
